title: HTML in Web Applications Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential concepts of modern web applications compared to traditional static websites.

questions.question: What is a 'Single-Page Application' (SPA)?
options: A website with only one single page of content.
A web application that dynamically loads and updates content without refreshing the entire browser page.
A page that can only be used by one person at a time.
An application that does not use any JavaScript.

questions.question: Which of these is the primary benefit of 'Server-Side Rendering' (SSR) compared to Client-Side Rendering (CSR)?
options: It uses less data.
It provides a faster 'first paint' and is generally better for SEO (Search Engine Optimization).
It allows users to use the website without an internet connection.
It removes the need for any HTML5 tags.

questions.question: What is the 'Virtual DOM' primarily used for in modern frameworks like React?
options: To provide a backup version of the site in case of a crash.
To efficiently track and batch updates before applying them to the real, slower browser DOM.
To allow users to view the site as if they were in a VR headset.
To store sensitive user data locally.

questions.question: Which 'PWA' (Progressive Web App) feature allows web apps to function without a stable internet connection?
options: Service Workers
Web Manifests
Cloud Storage
External Scripts

questions.question: Even in complex web applications, search engine crawlers and screen readers will ultimately see and interact with what?
options: Just the JavaScript source code.
The final, rendered HTML structure in the browser's real DOM.
A special JSON version of the page.
They cannot interact with dynamic applications at all.
